title: Doh In In The Wind

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes his car (inferred from tires screeching)
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's catchphrase is perfectly integrated with his character and economical, though not particularly original by this point in the series

jokes.text: One bouillon cube. One Concord grape. One Philly cheesesteak. And a jar of garlic pickles. No one will want to kiss me after these, eh, Smithers?
jokes.setup: Burns lists his bizarre lunch components
jokes.punchline: His concern about kissability reveals his delusion about romantic prospects
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific absurdism with the random lunch items and Burns' delusional self-perception

jokes.text: Well, it's their loss, sir.
jokes.setup: Burns' delusional comment about kissability
jokes.punchline: Smithers' diplomatic but absurd agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers response - diplomatic subservience masking the absurdity of the situation

jokes.text: No. Those people are all gristle. I want this jar open.
jokes.setup: Smithers suggests ordering Chinese food
jokes.punchline: Burns interprets 'Chinese' literally as eating Chinese people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that plays on Burns' evil nature and creates a shocking double-take moment with perfect economy

jokes.text: Worthless old geezers. Phew. Next!
jokes.setup: Burns struggles to open the pickle jar
jokes.punchline: He calls other people 'old geezers' while being ancient himself
jokes.explanation: Good character irony - Burns lacks self-awareness about his own extreme age

jokes.text: Would you like me to drain Simpson while he's passed out, sir?
jokes.setup: Burns mentions needing 'fresh blood'
jokes.punchline: Smithers offers to literally drain Homer's blood
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke - Smithers takes the metaphor literally in a perfectly character-appropriate way

jokes.text: A talkie, sir? Yes. Brilliant. That's just the kind of far-out gimmick we need.
jokes.setup: Burns suggests making a recruitment film
jokes.punchline: He thinks 'talkies' are cutting-edge technology
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns antiquated perspective - treating 1920s technology as revolutionary

jokes.text: What about chestnut roasting? People always need chestnuts. Or begging. I know a place that'll saw your legs off.
jokes.setup: Discussion about career options after college
jokes.punchline: Lenny suggests increasingly bizarre and dark career paths
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from the mundane (chestnuts) to the darkly absurd (voluntary leg amputation)

jokes.text: You dunderheaded stooges are the worst bunch of-
jokes.setup: Burns starts to berate the actors
jokes.punchline: He's cut off mid-insult when filming resumes
jokes.explanation: Good timing joke with Burns' characteristic insults interrupted by the filming process

jokes.text: Well, it's better than that last Barbra Streisand movie.
jokes.setup: Watching the terrible recruitment video
jokes.punchline: Moe's backhanded compliment comparing it to Streisand films
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference with Moe's characteristic dry delivery

jokes.text: I didn't quite get the point of it. Why would Lenny want someone to saw his legs off?
jokes.setup: References Lenny's earlier bizarre career suggestion
jokes.punchline: Barney's confusion about the leg-sawing career path
jokes.explanation: Good callback that maintains the absurdity of the original joke

jokes.text: I didn't choose to be a gifted actor, Marge. It chose me. I'm merely a vessel through which genius flows.
jokes.setup: Homer's terrible performance in the video
jokes.punchline: His grandiose delusion about his acting abilities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer delusion - contrasts his terrible performance with his inflated self-perception

jokes.text: Fat Guy Number Three?
jokes.setup: Homer creating fake acting credits
jokes.punchline: His 'phony' credit is actually painfully accurate
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor about Homer's physical appearance and likely casting type

jokes.text: You know, I have no idea... How should I know?
jokes.setup: Asked about his middle name
jokes.punchline: Homer doesn't know his own middle name despite having it for decades
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's lack of self-knowledge and general obliviousness

jokes.text: It was your mother's job to name ya and love ya and such. I was mainly in it for the spankin'.
jokes.setup: Explaining parental responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Grampa admits he was only interested in corporal punishment
jokes.explanation: Dark but character-perfect humor about Grampa's old-fashioned and somewhat disturbing parenting philosophy

jokes.text: Serves her right for bein' a '60s radical! Though she was a demon in the sack.
jokes.setup: Discussing Homer's mother being on the run
jokes.punchline: Grampa's inappropriate sexual comment about his ex-wife to their son
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor from Grampa oversharing sexual details with his son

jokes.text: No. Homer Simpson does not lie twice on the same form. He never has, and he never will.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to make up a middle name
jokes.punchline: Homer's arbitrary moral line about lying on forms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - creating an arbitrary moral boundary despite already lying

jokes.text: Yeah, but they were all part of a single ball of lies.
jokes.setup: Challenged about lying on mortgage application
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre distinction between multiple lies and 'a ball of lies'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic with the memorable phrase 'ball of lies' - perfectly captures his twisted reasoning

jokes.text: Great. I'll go shoot myself for bringing this up.
jokes.setup: Homer's enthusiasm about a father-son chat
jokes.punchline: Bart's extreme regret about starting this conversation
jokes.explanation: Good Bart reaction showing his immediate regret about getting involved in family drama

jokes.text: There's a pond for skinny dippin', a tire for skinny swingin'
jokes.setup: Homer exploring the hippie commune
jokes.punchline: The 'skinny' wordplay pattern applied to tire swinging
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke extending the 'skinny dipping' concept to other activities

jokes.text: I can actually feel the good vibrations. [Bee stings him] Ouch.
jokes.setup: Homer feeling the peaceful hippie vibes
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets stung by a bee
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic timing - peace and nature immediately conflict with reality

jokes.text: Your mother dragged us both to that godforsaken lovefest!
jokes.setup: Revelation that Grampa went to Woodstock
jokes.punchline: His characteristically negative description of the iconic festival
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Grampa's curmudgeonly perspective on hippie culture

jokes.text: Boo! Bring on Sha Na Na!
jokes.setup: Woodstock flashback with psychedelic rock
jokes.punchline: Grampa preferring the nostalgic 1950s revival act
jokes.explanation: Great period-specific joke about Woodstock featuring the more conservative act that actually performed there

jokes.text: Shame on you, boy! Put some damn pants on and then pull 'em down! 'Cause it's time for a spankin'!
jokes.setup: Young Homer dancing naked at Woodstock
jokes.punchline: Grampa's contradictory command about putting on pants just to remove them for punishment
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic with perfect character consistency - Grampa's obsession with spanking creates contradictory commands

jokes.text: Get a load of Captain Bring Down.
jokes.setup: Grampa ruining the hippie vibe
jokes.punchline: The hippie's perfectly accurate nickname for Grampa
jokes.explanation: Good period-appropriate nickname that perfectly captures Grampa's role in the scene

jokes.text: Never! What you need's a good long hitch in Vietnam! There must be an enlistment tent around here somewhere.
jokes.setup: Young Homer wanting to be a hippie
jokes.punchline: Grampa threatening to enlist him for the war hippies were protesting
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor about the generational conflict over Vietnam, perfectly in character for Grampa

jokes.text: If I'd have left it up to your mother... you'd have ended up in a hellhole like this... just lyin' around, never workin'... without a care in your head... full of long, luxurious hippie hair!
jokes.setup: Grampa listing the horrors of hippie life
jokes.punchline: His escalating 'complaints' sound increasingly appealing
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation where Grampa's attempts to make hippie life sound terrible actually make it sound wonderful

jokes.text: Lather, rinse and repeat. Always repeat.
jokes.setup: Hippie girls asking about his hair care
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation of shampoo bottle instructions
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Homer's literal-minded approach even as a young hippie

jokes.text: HomerJ., will you teach us to make love?
jokes.setup: Homer's apparent hippie success
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate request creates an uncomfortable moment
jokes.explanation: Standard uncomfortable humor about hippie free love culture

jokes.text: And a demon in the sack. [Both chuckling] Ah, you heard about that, eh?
jokes.setup: Seth mentioning Homer's mother
jokes.punchline: Awkward confirmation that everyone knew about their sex life
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable callback revealing that Homer's parents' sex life was commune knowledge

jokes.text: Oh, my God! My middle name is right behind that shrub. I'll finally know what 'J' stands for. From this moment forth, I will be known as Homer... Jay Simpson!
jokes.setup: Buildup to revealing Homer's mysterious middle name
jokes.punchline: The anticlimactic revelation that 'J' just stands for 'Jay'
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - builds up mystery only to reveal the most obvious and anticlimactic answer possible

jokes.text: Get off of there, Ginsberg!
jokes.setup: Dog on the mural
jokes.punchline: The hippie named his dog after beat poet Allen Ginsberg
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing the hippie's literary pretensions in pet naming

jokes.text: Uh, you might want to wash that. The dog has a lot of skin and bladder problems.
jokes.setup: Homer treasuring his mother's poncho
jokes.punchline: Revelation that it's been contaminated by a sick dog
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor that ruins Homer's sentimental moment

jokes.text: I'm gonna let my spirit soar... and love all of God's creatures. Get up, you stupid dog!
jokes.setup: Homer's hippie spiritual declaration
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by yelling at the dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Homer's inability to maintain hippie ideals for even seconds

jokes.text: Hey, peace, man. Far out. Groovy. I'm a hippie.
jokes.setup: Homer watching Bob Hope on TV to learn about hippies
jokes.punchline: Bob Hope's hilariously square attempt at hippie slang
jokes.explanation: Good period humor about mainstream entertainers trying to appeal to counterculture

jokes.text: I got you a date with the perfect flower child- Phyllis Diller.
jokes.setup: Setting up Bob Hope with a flower child
jokes.punchline: Phyllis Diller as the least flower-child-like person possible
jokes.explanation: Good casting joke contrasting the spiritual hippie ideal with Diller's aggressive comedy persona

jokes.text: Bummer. Love-in. Dean Rusk. By George, I've got it!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to understand hippie culture from Bob Hope
jokes.punchline: His random collection of words somehow gives him an epiphany
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic showing how his mind makes random connections and treats them as profound insights

jokes.text: Your potato? You can't, like, own a potato, man. It's one of Mother Earth's creatures.
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer to move away from her potato
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-philosophical hippie response about potato ownership
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist philosophy applied to mundane situation - perfectly captures shallow hippie rhetoric

jokes.text: No way, narc! Bodily functions are a natural thing. Not to mention hilarious.
jokes.setup: Marge asking him to excuse himself after belching
jokes.punchline: Homer's hippie justification for gross behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Homer using hippie philosophy to justify his existing bad habits

jokes.text: Like the whole shaving trip. Come on! I want to see those legs all furry and gross.
jokes.setup: Homer listing Marge's 'hang-ups'
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastically gross description of unshaven legs
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor about Homer's misguided attempts at hippie attitudes toward body hair

jokes.text: Free the Springfield two, Marge! Free the Springfield two!
jokes.setup: Homer wanting Marge to remove her bra
jokes.punchline: Reference to political prisoner movements but about Marge's breasts
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to political activism movements applied to domestic situation

jokes.text: Come on, Maude! The human wang is a beautiful thing.
jokes.setup: Maude screaming at seeing Homer naked
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate philosophical defense of male nudity
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor from Homer's oblivious pseudo-philosophical response to neighbor's distress

jokes.text: We've got everything we need right here. Good times. Sunshine. Free love.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering the perfect hippie paradise
jokes.punchline: The immediate reality check that it's actually a commercial operation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the disillusionment - Homer's idealistic vision about to be shattered by capitalism

jokes.text: Oh, we were on a break. Time is money, man.
jokes.setup: Homer asking why hippies are working
jokes.punchline: The corporate-speak motto delivered with hippie slang
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast between hippie language and capitalist values - captures the sell-out theme perfectly

jokes.text: Peter Fonda must be spinning in his grave.
jokes.setup: Homer's outrage at the commercialized hippies
jokes.punchline: Using the 'spinning in grave' phrase about someone who's still alive
jokes.explanation: Good Homer malapropism mixing up the idiom about dead people with living hippie icon Peter Fonda

jokes.text: Look. We can sit here all day and play the blame game... or we can start freaking people out!
jokes.setup: Homer deflecting from the hypocrisy accusation
jokes.punchline: His solution is more hippie activities rather than addressing the point
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's ability to deflect criticism and redirect energy

jokes.text: I drive a Saturn.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about their freak bus
jokes.punchline: The mundane replacement vehicle reveals how far they've fallen
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery showing the contrast between hippie ideals and suburban reality

jokes.text: In a way, the '60s ended the day we sold it- December 31, 1969.
jokes.setup: Explaining when they sold their bus
jokes.punchline: The symbolically perfect date for the death of the '60s
jokes.explanation: Clever historical observation linking personal sell-out to the literal end of the decade

jokes.text: Fine. I guess the juice business is more important than the ideals... our hippie forefathers refused to go to war and die for.
jokes.setup: Homer guilting them about prioritizing work
jokes.punchline: His backwards logic about what hippies 'died for' by avoiding death
jokes.explanation: Good twisted logic showing Homer's confused understanding of hippie history and martyrdom

jokes.text: Hey, we'll call it a business trip and write off the mileage.
jokes.setup: Agreeing to the freak-out
jokes.punchline: Immediately thinking about tax deductions for their hippie activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect sellout humor - can't even rebel without thinking about business expenses

jokes.text: It's that pair of Dockers you wanted. 48 waist with the balloon seat, right?
jokes.setup: Marge encountering Homer's hippie friends
jokes.punchline: Her public revelation of Homer's large pants size and unflattering style
jokes.explanation: Good embarrassing moment contrasting Homer's counterculture image with mundane reality

jokes.text: Fifteen years of loyal service, and this is how they tell me? A jester with an invisible proclamation.
jokes.setup: Plant being 'closed' by Homer's proclamation
jokes.punchline: Lenny's matter-of-fact acceptance of this absurd termination method
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan response treating the absurd situation as a legitimate workplace concern

jokes.text: Your Frisbee jammed the juice-ilator, Homer. Our entire shipment is ruined!
jokes.setup: Homer's frisbee causing industrial damage
jokes.punchline: The made-up machine name 'juice-ilator' and the destruction
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with the machine name and physical comedy consequence

jokes.text: Uh, uh, pretty, uh, freaky, huh?
jokes.setup: Homer causing serious business damage
jokes.punchline: His weak attempt to frame disaster as successful freaking out
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor from Homer's oblivious response to causing real damage

jokes.text: You're not a hippie, and you never were. Please, Homer... just leave us alone.
jokes.setup: Homer's hippie phase causing real problems
jokes.punchline: The brutal rejection destroying Homer's identity fantasy
jokes.explanation: Effective emotional beat showing consequences of Homer's destructive enthusiasm

jokes.text: How could you let me turn into you? Bu-Bu-But the poncho!
jokes.setup: Homer confronting his father about becoming like him
jokes.punchline: Both characters' pathetic focus on the symbolic poncho
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how both Simpson men fixate on superficial symbols

jokes.text: A spokesman attributed the production shutdown to a half-witted oaf.
jokes.setup: News report about the juice company shutdown
jokes.punchline: The accurate but harsh corporate description of Homer
jokes.explanation: Good corporate speak that's brutally accurate about Homer's role in the disaster

jokes.text: Ah, it was sweet of those guys to blame an oaf... but really it was my fault.
jokes.setup: Homer hearing the news report
jokes.punchline: His complete obliviousness that he IS the oaf they're blaming
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer obliviousness joke - he completely misses that he's the oaf being blamed

jokes.text: No, really. You're lazy and self-righteous. And the soles of your feet are jet-black.
jokes.setup: Homer thinking Lisa is just being nice about him being a good hippie
jokes.punchline: Lisa's brutally accurate list of why he actually fits the hippie stereotype
jokes.explanation: Great character moment where Lisa's honesty accidentally supports Homer's hippie delusions

jokes.text: But then I found the other garden behind the barn... the one with the camouflage netting.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how he got enough vegetables
jokes.punchline: Revealing he harvested their marijuana plants
jokes.explanation: Good reveal building to the drug-induced chaos that follows, with Homer's oblivious delivery

jokes.text: Well, now the whole town can benefit from their nutrients.
jokes.setup: Learning he harvested their marijuana
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent description of distributing drugs as 'nutrients'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - he genuinely doesn't understand what he's done and describes it in wholesome terms

jokes.text: Willie, you complete me.
jokes.setup: Groundskeeper Willie hallucinating from the marijuana juice
jokes.punchline: His romantic vision of his lawn mower
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific hallucination showing Willie's relationship with his equipment

jokes.text: Oh! Thanks, Pinky! You've always been there for me.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum hallucinating
jokes.punchline: Treating his gun as a loyal friend named Pinky
jokes.explanation: Good character hallucination showing Wiggum's inappropriate relationship with his weapon

jokes.text: Pucker up, Ned.
jokes.setup: Ned and Maude hallucinating
jokes.punchline: The ultra-conservative couple getting aggressively romantic in public
jokes.explanation: Good contrast humor showing the repressed Flanders becoming uninhibited

jokes.text: The electric yellow has got me by the brain banana.
jokes.setup: Lou describing his drug-induced state
jokes.punchline: The completely nonsensical but evocative description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant surreal description that captures drug-induced synesthesia in wonderfully absurd language

jokes.text: My God! It's nothin' but carrots and peyote. Damn longhairs never learn, Chief.
jokes.setup: Analyzing the juice ingredients
jokes.punchline: Lou's matter-of-fact delivery while completely high himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic moment where Lou maintains professional demeanor while obviously affected by the drugs

jokes.text: You can smash this drug barn all you want... but first you'll have to smash our heads open like ripe melons!
jokes.setup: Homer making a defiant stand
jokes.punchline: His overly graphic and disturbing description of police brutality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor with Homer's characteristically graphic and inappropriate imagery

jokes.text: Uh, mine's stuck on twirl. Oh, for the love of- There.
jokes.setup: Police setting nightsticks to 'whomp'
jokes.punchline: Technical difficulties with police brutality equipment
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor treating nightsticks like household appliances with settings

jokes.text: Look at yourselves. Pointing guns at your fellow man. Hatred is a cage that keeps us from soaring free.
jokes.setup: Police about to shoot
jokes.punchline: Homer's sudden sincere hippie wisdom in a life-threatening situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer finally delivering genuine hippie philosophy when it matters

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer getting hit by a flower in the gun
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation after the anticlimactic injury
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase bookending the episode, though not particularly creative by this point

jokes.text: If that had been a gladiola, he'd be dead right now.
jokes.setup: Doctor examining Homer's flower injury
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific medical assessment about flower lethality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist medical expertise delivered with complete authority - perfect Simpsons surreal humor

jokes.text: I'm a doctor, not a gardener.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to pull out the flower
jokes.punchline: Star Trek reference applied to a ridiculous medical situation
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits the absurd medical situation

jokes.text: I buried Flanders.
jokes.setup: Homer humming and looking content
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of apparent murder while relaxing
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark ending line delivered with casual contentment - excellent contrast and memorable closer

tmdb_overview: In a quest to learn Homer's middle name, Homer and Grandpa travel to his mother's old commune, where Homer embraces the hippie life.
insight: Homer adopts a vagrant, delivering 73 jokes at 3.53 per minute with zero standout moments.
